sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to break out, to burst forth.-(a) used of the young, as issuing from the womb (Arab. فرخ; compare at the root פָּרַד); whence אֶפְרֹוחַ, פִּרְחָח.-(b) to sprout, to flourish, to bud forth, as a plant, Isa 17:11 Job 14:9 to put forth buds, leaves, flowers, as a tree (ausfchlagen), Son 6:11 Hab 3:17. Metaph.-(α) used of the flourishing and prosperous condition of a person or nation, Psa 92:8, 13 Psa 92:13 Isa 27:6.-(β) Hos 10:4, “punishment shall bud forth like the poppy.”-(c) to break out, as a leprosy, sore (Germ. ausfchlagen), Lev 13:12, seqq.; 14:43 Exo 9:9, 10 .

(2) to fly, like the Chald., Eze 13:20 (How this signification can be reconciled with that of budding forth, see under the syn. נָצַץ .)

Hiphil-

(1) to cause to bud forth, or flourish (said of God), Isa 17:11 Eze 17:24.

(2) to put forth (to make) buds and flowers, as a tree, Psa 92:13 Job 14:9. Metaph. Pro 14:11.

Derivatives, פֶּרַח, פִּרְחָח, אֶפְרֹחַ, and pr.n. פָּרוּחַ.
